title: Putting it all together
subtitle: A practical review across the full curriculum

vocabulary.exampleCyrillic: Какие у вас планы на выходные?
vocabulary.exampleTransliteration: Kakiye u vas plany na vikhodnyye?
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: What are your plans for the weekend?
I have a meeting on Wednesday.

hook.text: This is the final lesson of the curriculum. You've covered the full Russian A1 system: the alphabet, core grammar (cases, aspect, verbs, adjectives), and everyday language. This lesson doesn't introduce new grammar — it integrates everything into realistic, multi-structure communication. The goal: produce connected Russian sentences about your life without thinking about individual rules.

why.text: Language production is harder than recognition. Each previous lesson drilled one structure at a time. Real speech mixes them: introducing yourself requires zero copula, possessives, adjective agreement, and prepositional case in the same paragraph. This lesson forces that integration deliberately, across the areas where combination is hardest.

cards.title: Self-introduction — a complete template
cards.body: Here is a full self-introduction paragraph combining vocabulary and structures from across the curriculum. Every sentence draws on a different unit. Study this as a model — then adapt it to your own details.

examples.meaning: My name is Orfan. (Unit 2)
I am from London. (из + genitive, Unit 5)
I am 25 years old. (dative age, Units 5 + 8)

examples.cyrillic: Я студент. Я изучаю русский язык.
examples.transliteration: Ya student. Ya izuchayu russkiy yazyk.
examples.meaning: I am a student. I study Russian. (zero copula; verb + accusative, Units 2 + 4)
I live in London. (жить + prepositional, Units 4 + 5)

cards.title: Talking about your week
cards.body: Combining days of the week, time expressions, and event vocabulary into a connected description of your schedule. Each sentence uses a different case or construction.

examples.meaning: On Monday I have a lesson. (в + acc. day; у меня есть)

examples.cyrillic: Во вторник и в среду я работаю.
examples.transliteration: Vo vtornik i v sredu ya rabotayu.
examples.meaning: On Tuesday and Wednesday I work. (two days + verb)

examples.cyrillic: В четверг встреча в три часа.
examples.transliteration: V chetverg vstrecha v tri chasa.
examples.meaning: On Thursday there's a meeting at three o'clock. (day + time)
On Friday I am free. (short-form adjective)
On weekends I rest.

cards.title: Describing someone — a complete paragraph
cards.body: A model paragraph about another person, integrating family vocabulary, adjective agreement, profession, location, and age from Units 7-8.

examples.meaning: This is my sister Anna. (possessive + noun)
She is 32. (dative age: ей + number + год zone)
She is clever and cheerful. (adjective agreement: feminine)
Anna is a doctor. She works as a doctor. (identity vs role)
She lives in Moscow. (prepositional)

questions.prompt: How do you say 'tell me about yourself' (formal)?
questions.explanation: рассказать → formal imperative = расскажите. о себе = about yourself (prepositional of себя).

questions.prompt: On Wednesday I have a meeting: В ___ у меня встреча.
questions.explanation: среда → среду (feminine accusative: А→У). В среду.
Last digit 2 → года (genitive singular). Ей тридцать два года.

cards.title: Common combination errors — to watch for
cards.body: These are the errors most likely to occur when combining structures. Review them carefully — the tutor has tracked these throughout your lessons.

examples.cyrillic: У меня есть сестра. / У меня нет сестры.
examples.transliteration: U menya yest' sestra / net sestry.
examples.meaning: есть + nominative / нет + genitive — don't mix these.

examples.cyrillic: Я иду в магазин. / Я в магазине.
examples.transliteration: Ya idu v magazin. / Ya v magazine.
examples.meaning: Direction: в + accusative. Location: в + prepositional. Same preposition, different case.
Identity: nominative. Role/manner: instrumental.

examples.cyrillic: Я читал книгу. / Я прочитал книгу.
examples.transliteration: Ya chital knigu. / Ya prochital knigu.
examples.meaning: Was reading (process) vs read and finished. Aspect matters.

cards.title: Where to go from here
cards.body: You have completed A1 and are entering solid A2 territory. The next priorities: (1) expand vocabulary in domains you care about, (2) work on declension fluency — all six cases without stopping to think, (3) aspect in speech — choosing automatically. Use this app's tutor for any questions that come up in conversation.

examples.meaning: Keep studying! (imperfective imperative — ongoing action)
You already know a lot.

questions.prompt: I am from London: Я ___ Лондона.
questions.explanation: из + genitive = from. Я из Лондона.

questions.prompt: She is clever and cheerful (feminine adjectives): Она умная и ___.
questions.explanation: весёлый → весёлая (feminine). Она умная и весёлая.

questions.prompt: On Friday I am free (masculine short form): В пятницу я ___.
What are your plans?: Какие у вас ___?
questions.explanation: планы = plans (plural of план). Какие у вас планы?

questions.prompt: I have a meeting on Wednesday: У меня ___ в среду.
questions.explanation: встреча = meeting. У меня встреча в среду.

questions.prompt: She works as a doctor: Она работает ___.
I read (process, yesterday): Вчера я ___ книгу.
questions.explanation: Context: process, all day. But 'yesterday I read all day' = ongoing process → imperfective (читал). If 'finished it' → прочитал.

rules: Zero copula: Я студент. Он врач. — no 'is' in present tense
в + accusative (direction/time) / в + prepositional (location/month) — same preposition, different case
Identity (nominative) vs role (instrumental): Он врач / Он работает врачом
Aspect: imperfective = process/habit; perfective = completed single event
summary.nextLesson: Curriculum complete. You have covered A1 → solid A2. Use the tutor for conversation practice and explore the vocabulary list to consolidate what you've learned.

questions.prompt: I am from Moscow: Я ___ Москвы.
She works as a teacher: Она работает ___.
I don't have a sister: У меня нет ___.
I was reading yesterday (process): Вчера я ___.
